title: Hash Tables, Dictionaries, Sets, and Collision Policy · Complexity Forensics
summary: Create a deduplication and lookup service that remains correct under collision-heavy data. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a hash-table model and workload benchmark and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Relate hash stability, equality, and immutable key requirements.
Trace separate chaining and open addressing through collision and deletion cases.
Explain how load factor and resizing influence expected cost and memory.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
